current_status: Formal project F4 MinPlayableLoop manual playtest fixes landed on 2026-06-18: after user playtest feedback, the F4 runtime now shows player aim direction, flashes player/enemies on hit, draws a world grid background for movement reference, freezes GameClock in GAME_OVER, and extends the early chaser wave to 600s / high budget so the first loop does not stop around 40 kills. `python tools/godot_bridge.py --project client f4-smoke` asserts F4RunLoop mounting, F4Background mounting, GameState PLAYING, WASD / arrow aim separation, player movement, arrow aiming without movement, bullet pool acquisition, enemy spawning, Combat damage against enemies and player, death -> GameState.GAME_OVER, and GameClock freeze in GAME_OVER. The runtime covers player movement, centered Camera2D, default starting_loadout weapon grant, minimal WeaponSystem, pooled bullets, pooled chaser enemies, spawn_waves.csv-driven spawning, Combat.apply_damage via DamageInfo, a minimal HUD, and death -> pause-action restart. Combat is registered as an autoload; F4 runtime details live in docs/代码/f4_min_playable_loop.md and docs/代码/combat.md. Stage 1 gates, F4 smoke, headless boot, export-tree, JSON validation, docs health, and diff check have passed in the current playtest-fix slice. tools/lint_semantic_rules.py may report expected non-blocking add_child advisory warnings for F4 scene/HUD/background assembly and reparenting nodes acquired from PoolManager; these are accepted for the current script-assembled F4 slice and should be revisited when scenes/UI are formalized. F4 remains scoped to a 1-minute playable combat loop; do not jump ahead to F5+ pause save/resume, deep meta progression, full upgrade UI, golden replay stability, balance sim, networking, or large content expansion.
last_completed: AI knowledge base v2 with docs_health_check
Experience and level-up choice system decision (ADR #43)
AI memory split into long-term index, current_state.json, and session logs (ADR #44)
Historical MVP M4.6 centralized player, input, weapon, enemy, spawner, and background tunables before the MVP sandbox was removed
Full project numeric and localization configuration manuals established in client/data/README.md and client/locale/README.md (ADR #45)
Full project deep meta progression specified with meta currency, account level, permanent upgrades, unlocks, and SaveManager meta persistence boundary (ADR #46)
Human-readable future task backlog established in docs/TODO.md
Minimal Stage 1 CI enabled in .github/workflows/docs-check.yml for JSON, CSV, docs health, and whitespace checks (ADR #47)

Strong player save requirement established: SaveManager must support meta progression saves and run suspend/resume saves with migration, atomic writes, backup fallback, and broken-save quarantine (ADR #48)

Seven creative/strategy subagents added across CodeBuddy, Codex, and OpenCode: game-designer, numeric-designer, ip-designer, copywriter-packager, ui-art-designer, game-art-designer, marketing-strategist (ADR #49)

Communication rule added: when asked for issues/risks, answer factually and do not invent problems; when new requirements arrive, first assess project fit, cost-effectiveness, complexity, and risks (ADR #50)

Data validation, contract sync, and lightweight Godot Bridge landed: tools/sync_contracts.py, tools/validate_data.py, tools/godot_bridge.py, generated _contracts.json and client/scripts/contracts/*.gd, and docs-check CI now runs sync + validate + docs health (ADR #51)
AI Git commit policy established: large changes are auto-committed after status/diff/log checks and verification; minor changes are not auto-committed; only task-owned files may be staged (ADR #52)

Eight project-level skills initially installed under .opencode/skills and now synchronized across .codebuddy/skills, .codex/skills, and .opencode/skills for Godot/GDScript, scene validation, Godot test diagnostics, doc sync, safe git commit, factual code review, AI resource curation, and MCP tool evaluation (ADR #53)

Context-compaction task recovery rule established: after summaries or context restoration, user last explicit instruction overrides summaries, Next Steps, and candidate current_state actions (ADR #54)
Historical external AI resource submodules were installed for review (ADR #55; later superseded by ADR #59)
Historical external AI tools were briefly installed while excluding templates/examples (ADR #56; later superseded by ADR #58 and #59)
Historical CCGS cross-agent adapter was added for on-demand reuse (ADR #57; later superseded by ADR #58 and #59)
Historical external AI library active layer was simplified to a reference adapter (ADR #58; later superseded by ADR #59)

Project-level skills consolidated from vendor/reference into direct project skills: useful GodotPrompter, headless-godot-skill-kit, and CCGS patterns were absorbed; .agents/skills/game-ai-reference, vendor submodules, and .gitmodules were removed (ADR #59)

Nine project-level skills are synchronized across .codebuddy/skills, .codex/skills, and .opencode/skills so CodeBuddy, Codex, and OpenCode can each read direct same-name skills without cross-platform references (ADR #60)

MVP M4.7 added debug/dev_tools-only GM console with stats, HP, damage, spawn, clear/kill, spawner toggle, and reset commands; full project rule requires release presets to exclude debug tools (ADR #61)
User confirmed MVP validation and AI work framework setup are complete; formal full-project roadmap was established in docs/正式项目工作规划.md (ADR #62)
Numeric configuration format policy established: flat table values prefer CSV, complex configuration prefers JSON, and DataLoader should support both (ADR #63)
Initial localization scope established: zh_CN and en are the first two maintained languages, and AI should auto-fill the missing counterpart as a first-draft translation for human review (ADR #64)

Multi-mode resource reuse principle established: resource definitions stay mode-agnostic by default, while future game mode configs compose resource pools, weights, conditions, blocklists, and light overrides (ADR #65)

Future multiplayer PvE/PvP boundary established: do not implement networking now, but avoid hardcoding a single player or fixed player-vs-enemy assumptions across input, Combat, teams, Replay, SaveManager, analytics, and game mode config (ADR #66)
Formal project F1 skeleton established: client/project.godot, minimal boot scene/script, standard client directories, client README, and FormalClientBoot module documentation.
Formal project F2 first autoload slice established: DataLoader, RNG, GameState, and GameClock are registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 second autoload slice established: Settings, Localization, and UIManager are registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 Analytics autoload slice established: Analytics is registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 Replay autoload slice established: Replay is registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 PoolManager autoload slice established: PoolManager is registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 SaveManager autoload slice established: SaveManager is registered, boot-smoked, roundtrip-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 AudioManager autoload slice established: AudioManager is registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
AI quick-start context path established: docs/AI协作/快速开工.md is the default low-token startup route and long-term memory is cold storage (ADR #67).
F3 DataLoader work package established and code-stage reading now prioritizes module docs plus target source (ADR #68).
Large code changes now require an automatic factual code review before commit, while minor changes do not trigger formal review (ADR #69).

Formal project F3 DataLoader schema regression tests established: tools/test_data_loader_schema.py now covers golden data, unknown ids, missing locale keys, wrong types, invalid ranges, and cross-file references and is included in Stage 1 CI (ADR #70).

Formal project F3 first DataLoader schema slice established: DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate player.json, meta_progression.json, strings.csv, growth.csv, and growth_pools.json; growth golden examples are documented.

Formal project F3 game_modes schema boundary established: game_modes.json, generated GameModes constants, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the standard survival mode resource pools, participants, teams, blocklists, and lightweight overrides without implementing mode runtime.

Formal project F3 characters schema boundary established: characters.json, character locale keys, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default character base stats, tags, capabilities, control profile, and cross-file references from game_modes/meta unlocks.

Formal project F3 character starting_loadout schema boundary established: characters.json, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default character starting weapon, active item, and consumable references without implementing runtime grants.

Formal project F3 weapons schema boundary established: weapons.json, weapon locale keys, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default weapon base stats, projectile pool, damage type, fire mode, fire audio id, character starting_loadout weapon references, and game_modes weapon pool references.

Formal project F3 relics schema boundary established: relics.json, relic locale keys, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default relic tags, modifiers, behaviors, and game_modes relic pool references without implementing relic runtime.

Formal project F3 enemies schema boundary established: enemies.csv, enemy locale key, tag_enemy contract, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default enemy tags, pool id, contact damage type, base stats, and game_modes enemy pool references without implementing enemy runtime or spawning.

Formal project F3 hazards schema boundary established: hazards.csv, hazard locale key, tag_hazard contract, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default hazard tags, pool id, damage type, trigger interval, radius, duration, and game_modes hazard pool references without implementing HazardSystem runtime, collision, or damage.

Formal project F3 spawn_waves schema boundary established: spawn_waves.csv, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default wave mode reference, wave index, time window, enemy reference, enemy weight, spawn interval, max alive, spawn budget, and optional hazard reference / weight without implementing Spawner runtime or enemy spawning.

Formal project F3 active_items schema boundary established: active_items.json, item locale keys, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default active item tag, cooldown charge, start/max charges, use effects, and game_modes active item pool references without implementing ActiveItemSystem runtime.

Formal project F3 consumables schema boundary established: consumables.json, item locale keys, DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate the default consumable tag, max stack, start count, pickup count, use effects, and game_modes consumable pool references without implementing ConsumableSystem runtime.

Dual credits lists established (ADR #71): root CREDITS.md is the repository-facing acknowledgements list, while client/data/credits.json is the in-game Credits UI data source validated by DataLoader and tools/validate_data.py.

affaan-m/ECC evaluated and partially absorbed (ADR #72): context budget, skill stocktake, strategic compact, verification loop, and GateGuard / harness-audit ideas were rewritten into the project-owned ai-resource-curator AI surface audit workflow; ECC is not installed as a harness, plugin, MCP, hooks runtime, dashboard, vendor tree, or bulk agent/skill/command pack.

affaan-m/ECC full README and tool-surface sweep recorded (ADR #73): docs/AI协作/ECC工具吸收清单.md now classifies 67 agents, 92 commands, 271 skills, hooks, scripts, MCP configs, and platform adapters, with reusable workflow patterns absorbed into ai-resource-curator and context-budget guidance.

Official Godot 4.6 GDScript style guide adopted as the project code style baseline (ADR #74): new/touched GDScript follows official naming, code order, formatting, comments, and typing guidance while stricter project rules still win and unrelated scripts are not bulk reformatted.
MVP sandbox directory removed after user-confirmed validation completion (ADR #75); tools/godot_bridge.py now defaults to the formal client and tools/validate_data.py no longer validates MVP config.

First-tier GDScript project lint established in Stage 1 CI (ADR #76): tools/lint_gdscript_rules.py checks low-false-positive section order, ambiguous := inference, Chinese hardcoded strings, and raw random / time tick / pause APIs.

Second-tier project rules lint established in Stage 1 CI (ADR #77): tools/lint_project_rules.py checks client/data/README.md field coverage, locale zh_CN / en bilingual coverage, and release preset debug/dev_tools exclusion when export_presets.cfg exists.

Third-tier semantic advisory lint established in Stage 1 CI (ADR #78): tools/lint_semantic_rules.py reports non-blocking warnings for special id branches, business-script autoload bypass, missing type signatures, missing # Doc headers, and unknown generated contract constants.

Local pre-commit and tool-first review SOP established (ADR #79): .pre-commit-config.yaml runs Stage 1 checks locally, tools/check_staged_whitespace.py checks staged whitespace, and docs/AI协作/代码审核流程.md tells reviewers to inspect tool output before LLM diff review.

Formal project F3 DataLoader schema closure audited and accepted: all F3 target data files, cross-file references, schema regression tests, docs health, and Godot headless boot passed; F4-MinPlayableLoop work package is now the current stage entry.

Formal project F4 MinPlayableLoop first runtime slice landed: Combat autoload, DamageInfo, F4 runtime, player movement, centered camera, default weapon, pooled bullets/enemies, spawn_waves spawning, minimal HUD, and death/restart are wired into the formal client startup path.

Formal project F4 headless runtime smoke landed: `python tools/godot_bridge.py --project client f4-smoke` now verifies the core F4 runtime chain through movement, aiming, pooling, spawning, Combat damage, and GAME_OVER.
Formal project F4 manual playtest fixes landed: aim direction, hit flash feedback, world-grid movement reference, GAME_OVER GameClock freeze, and longer/high-budget first spawn wave.
next_actions: Manually rerun the formal client for a 1-minute F4 combat smoke: specifically confirm aim direction visibility, player/enemy hit flashes, background movement reference, GAME_OVER timer freeze, ongoing enemy spawning past 40 kills, HUD, and pause-action restart.

If the rerun still feels flat, tune only F4 feedback and data values first: player/enemy colors, hit flash readability, grid contrast, weapon fire rate/damage, enemy speed/HP, spawn interval, and max_alive.

Keep `python tools/godot_bridge.py --project client f4-smoke` in the F4 validation loop and extend it only for narrow runtime invariants; add GUT-side tests when the Godot test runner is introduced, starting with Combat.apply_damage, Player receive_damage, and Spawner wave timing.
Consider formal scene resources for Player / Bullet / Enemy / HUD after the script-assembled slice is stable; keep high-frequency entity lifecycle routed through PoolManager.

Keep F4 scoped to a 1-minute playable combat loop; defer F5+ pause save/resume, full menus, deep meta progression, full upgrade UI, golden replay stability, balance sim, networking, or large content expansion.

notes: Long-term decisions remain authoritative in docs/决策记录.md.
Use python tools/sync_contracts.py after changing docs/词表与契约.md, then run --check.

When adding staff, third-party libraries, external resources, assets, fonts, audio, templates, or AI workflow references, update both CREDITS.md and client/data/credits.json; player-visible credits labels use ui_credits_* locale keys.
Do not over-optimize or invent findings when the user asks for issues; say no issue when no actual issue is found.
When the user proposes a requirement, briefly assess project fit, value, cost-effectiveness, complexity, and major risks before implementing.
Detailed daily history remains in docs/AI记忆/会话日志/.
Do not read or modify draft/ or DRAFT/ unless explicitly authorized.

For large AI changes, create a git commit after checking status, diff, recent log, and verification; for large code changes, run factual code review before commit; for minor changes, explain why no commit was made and skip formal review.

Before formal code review, run pre-commit run --all-files when available or the equivalent Stage 1 scripts; reviewers should triage tools/lint_semantic_rules.py advisory warnings, then inspect only task-relevant diff and residual risks instead of re-checking lint-covered rules with an LLM.

Project-level skills live in synchronized .codebuddy/skills, .codex/skills, and .opencode/skills; source/evaluation notes live in docs/AI协作/AI技能资源评估.md; restart/reopen the relevant AI tool after changing skills paths or config.

After adding or absorbing external AI resources, use ai-resource-curator's AI surface audit: inventory three-platform AI files, classify keep/improve/merge/retire/reject, estimate context footprint, check duplication, verify gates, and confirm no active external hooks/MCP/plugins/vendor trees were introduced.
GM/debug tooling must stay behind debug/dev_tools build gates; release presets must not enable dev_tools or include debug scripts/GM command resources.
External AI resource packages are not kept as vendor submodules; useful external procedures must be absorbed into project-owned skills and project rules remain authoritative.

For large external AI tool repos, record a source snapshot and full tool-surface audit first; absorb useful procedure into project-owned docs/skills, and keep hooks, MCP, CLI, dashboards, plugins, and vendor trees uninstalled unless explicitly authorized.

After context summary, compaction, or restoration, align to the user's last explicit instruction first; summaries, Next Steps, and current_state next_actions are candidate context only, not authorization.
Default AI startup is now the low-token hot path: AGENTS.md -> docs/AI协作/快速开工.md -> current_state.json -> platform rules -> task-relevant AI导航 sections; read long-term memory only when needed.
Formal project stage work should prefer docs/AI协作/工作包/; current F3 entry is docs/AI协作/工作包/F3-DataLoader.md.
Code-stage tasks should read docs/代码文档规范.md, the relevant docs/代码/<module_id>.md, and target source first; read GDD/ADR only for design conflict, unclear semantics, or new decisions.

For new or touched GDScript, follow the official Godot 4.6 GDScript style guide for naming, code order, formatting, comments, and typing; stricter project rules still take precedence, run python tools/lint_gdscript_rules.py for the first-tier project lint, and broad reformatting of unrelated old scripts requires an explicit task.

For new or touched formal GDScript, run python tools/lint_semantic_rules.py to collect third-tier non-blocking semantic advisory warnings; review warnings manually before deciding whether to fix or allow.

For data, locale, or export preset changes, run python tools/lint_project_rules.py for the second-tier project rules lint; it complements validate_data.py and sync_contracts.py --check rather than replacing them.
Use python tools/godot_bridge.py for formal client scene-tree export, data validation, Godot version checks, and headless boot; the default project is client/.
